InfoLoomTwo.WorkforcePanel[TotalTooltip]: Total population at this education level
InfoLoomTwo.WorkforcePanel[UnemployedToo: Citizens actively looking for work but currently unemployed
InfoLoomTwo.WorkforcePanel[EmployableToo: Citizens of working age who are available for employment
InfoLoomTwo.WorkforcePanel[UnderTooltip]: Citizens who are working below the education level (Under employed)
InfoLoomTwo.WorkforcePanel[OutsideToolti: Citizens who are working outside of the city
InfoLoomTwo.WorkplacesPanel[TotalTooltip: Total workplaces at this education level
InfoLoomTwo.WorkplacesPanel[CommuterTool: Workplaces filled by commuters from outside the city
InfoLoomTwo.WorkplacesPanel[FilledToolti: Percentage of workplace positions that are filled
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[TotalProperties: Total number of residential properties by density type
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[EmptyProperties: Available properties ready for new households to move in. Green indicates sufficient supply, red indicates shortage.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[NoDemandAtToolt: Target number of empty properties needed to maintain zero building demand. Based on free residential requirement percentages.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[BuildingDemandT: Demand for new residential buildings. Calculated as (1 - empty properties / target empty properties) × 100. Green = surplus, Red = shortage. Total column shows overall empty property percentage.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[StudyPositionsT: Total available study positions across all education levels (elementary through university)
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[HappinessToolti: Average happiness of all citizens. Affects residential demand - unhappy citizens create more demand for housing.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[UnemploymentToo: Percentage of working-age population that is unemployed. High unemployment increases residential demand as people seek cheaper housing.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[HouseholdDemand: Overall demand for households to move into the city. Positive values indicate growing population, negative values indicate population decline.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[HouseholdsToolt: Total number of households that have recently moved into the city
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[HomelessTooltip: Number of households without homes. High homelessness increases residential demand and reduces city attractiveness.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[TaxRateTooltip]: Weighted average residential tax rate across all density types. Higher taxes reduce residential demand as people seek more affordable cities.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[StudentChanceTo: Percentage chance that unemployed citizens will become students instead of seeking jobs. Based on student vs unemployment demand factors.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[EmptyBuildingsT: Number of empty industrial/office buildings available for new companies to move in
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[PropertylessCom: Companies that exist but don't have a building to operate from. High numbers indicate need for more zoned land.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[LocalDemandTool: Production capacity compared to local demand. 100% means production equals demand. Higher values indicate overproduction.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[InputUtilizatio: How well input resources are being utilized by manufacturing. 110% is neutral, values capped at 400%.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[EmployeeCapacit: Ratio of current employees to maximum employee capacity. 72% industrial and 75% office are neutral ratios.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[WorkforceToolti: Available educated and uneducated workers that can be employed by new companies
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[StorageTooltip]: Storage facilities for industrial goods. The game spawns warehouses when demand for storage exists.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[StorageDescript: The game will spawn warehouses when DEMANDED TYPES exist.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[StorageEmptyBui: Empty warehouse buildings available for storage companies
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[DemandedTypesTo: Number of resource types that need storage capacity based on demand vs storage capacity
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[NoDemandTooltip: Resources that currently have no company demand due to oversupply, lack of workforce, or high taxes
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[ServiceUtilizat: Percentage of service capacity currently utilized. Higher utilization increases demand for more commercial services. Below 30% is considered insufficient demand.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[ProductionCapac: Current production capacity relative to resource needs. 100% means supply matches demand. Above 100% indicates oversupply which reduces demand for new commercial businesses.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[AvailableWorkfo: Number of citizens available for work in commercial businesses. More available workers increases demand for new businesses.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[EducatedWorkfor: Number of educated citizens (high school or higher) available for work.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[UneducatedWorkf: Number of uneducated citizens available for work.
InfoLoomTwo.DemandPanels[IncludedResourc: Resources that currently have no demand in your city. This may be due to oversupply, lack of customers, or economic factors.
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ResourceHead: Click to sort by resource name
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[BuyCostHeade: Cost to purchase resources from outside connections. Click to sort.
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[SellCostHead: Price received when selling resources to outside connections. Click to sort.
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ProfitHeader: Profit per unit (Sell Price - Buy Price). Positive values are profitable. Click to sort.
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ProfitMargin: Profit margin percentage ((Profit / Buy Cost) * 100). Higher is better. Click to sort.
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ImportAmount: Amount of resources imported from outside connections per ton. Click to sort.
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ExportAmount: Amount of resources exported to outside connections per ton. Click to sort.
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[BuyCostToolt: Cost to buy from outside connections:
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[SellCostTool: Price received when selling to outside connections:
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ProfitToolti: Profit per unit:  (Sell Price - Buy Price)
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ImportAmount: Import amount per ton:  (Total Import Amount / Count)
InfoLoomTwo.TradeCostsPanel[ExportAmount: Export amount per ton:  (Total Export Amount / Count)
InfoLoomTwo.CompanyPanels[VehiclesToolti: Current vehicle count vs maximum vehicle capacity
InfoLoomTwo.CompanyPanels[MoneyTooltip]: Current amount of money the company has
InfoLoomTwo.CompanyPanels[InputsTooltip]: The resource that goes into the company for the company to sell or make another product from
InfoLoomTwo.CompanyPanels[OutputsTooltip: The resource that comes out of the company to sell(Commercials) or use for another company(Industrials)
InfoLoomTwo.CompanyPanels[MaintenanceToo: The resources that are used to maintain the building
InfoLoomTwo.CompanyPanels[ServiceUsageTo: Current service usage as a percent of maximum
InfoLoomTwo.CompanyPanels[ProcessingTool: Input and output resources processed by this company in the production chain
InfoLoomTwo.Common[LegendTooltipSuffix]: Click on bar segments above to see detailed breakdown
